reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails strictly on the dimension of Content Accuracy. The user explicitly requested to read a specific sentence: '周末的天气真好，我们约好去郊外骑行，感受大自然的清新空气。一路上有说有笑，烦恼全都抛到脑后了，心情真是无比舒畅！'. However, Model 1 generated a completely different, improvised script ('周末的天气真是棒极了，阳光暖暖的...', extending to 28 seconds with flower descriptions, honey water metaphors, etc.). While the improvised content has a happy tone, the model failed to follow the core instruction of synthesizing the specific target text. According to the scoring standard, 'Voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script' results in a score of 0. Consequently, no credit can be given for the rhythm of the wrong text.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 strictly follows the Content Accuracy requirement, reading the text exactly as requested without omission or addition ('00:00 - 00:09'). Regarding the target feature '轻快的节奏' (Brisk/Lighthearted Rhythm), the audio delivers the lines with an upbeat and moderately fast pace that matches the happy context of the weekend outing. The duration (~9-10 seconds for ~45 characters) indicates a brisk speaking rate suitable for excitement. The intonation at the end of sentences (e.g., '心情真是无比舒畅') lifts appropriately, conveying the 'lighthearted' feeling. The flow is natural and fluent.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 hallucinated a completely different script, failing the most basic requirement of text-to-speech generation (Content Accuracy), which automatically disqualifies it with a score of 0 according to the guidelines. Model 2 followed the text perfectly and successfully implemented the 'brisk rhythm' requested by the prompt.
